nodes.name: bill-model.md
nodes.summary: Pins down the bill data schema: structured articles with topic tags, a stanceVector projection used for the dot-product vote shortcut, a stage/committee workflow, an amendment version chain, and session-bound expiry instead of tick-based deadlines.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds a Convex/TypeScript `defineTable` schema block inside Traditional Chinese prose; stanceVector is designed as a precomputed vector so per-agent voting avoids a full LLM re-read of the bill text.

nodes.name: external-event-model.md
nodes.summary: Defines two deliberately separate external-injection channels — Admin Override (research tool, bypasses the media layer) and World Event (enters the Event Stream and is subject to media framing) — with a shared, auditable externalEvents schema and a PoC scoped to admin_override only.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `processedAtTick: number | null` instead of a boolean specifically to preserve reproducibility/audit value, a deliberate tradeoff called out in the doc's own tradeoff table.

nodes.name: llm-cost-model.md
nodes.summary: Classifies LLM calls into A/B/C tiers, defines a dot-product shortcut for decideVote, a three-layer Reflect throttle, a prompt-caching strategy for the ~4.5K-token skillMd block, and concrete per-scale cost estimates (PoC ~$0.09 to 100-agent cycle ~$112) with a budget-cap degradation ladder.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes Python pseudocode (throttle logic, cost formulas) and a TypeScript LLM_CONFIG constants block with concrete Haiku/Sonnet pricing tables to justify each degradation threshold numerically.

nodes.name: media-model.md
nodes.summary: Specifies a three-channel opinion model (Media, Pollster, Campaign) where only polls are a noisy public snapshot of a hidden true opinionState, defines the sentiment/confidence update formulas per channel, a rule-based media_tick scoring/framing algorithm, and the final media_spotlight decay formula feeding the pressure function.
nodes.languageNotes: v2 revision that explicitly supersedes design-priority-three.md §3; separates 'true sentiment' (opinionState) from 'observed snapshot' (polls with sampling noise) so agents strategically react to imperfect information rather than ground truth.

nodes.name: observability-model.md
nodes.summary: Defines three non-overlapping observability schemas — llmCallLog (cost/debug), tickSnapshot (engineering health), stanceDriftLog (behavioral acceptance) — plus the behavior_consistency_score and stance_drift_analysis PoC acceptance metrics and the checkBudget/alerting logic that feeds the cost model's degradation thresholds.
nodes.languageNotes: checkBudget() TypeScript function is the direct numeric source for the llm-cost-model.md degradation ladder, illustrating a tight two-way contract between the docs rather than one-way dependency.

nodes.name: politician-skill-contract.md
nodes.summary: Locks the field-mapping contract from the external Politician-skill repo's meta.json/nodes.json/edges.json outputs into simulation Agent schema, including the backbone-derivation formula, the lossy hidden.social estimation, Zep relationship-graph seeding, and a version-update procedure when profileHash changes mid-simulation.
nodes.languageNotes: Explicitly reconciles a unit-scale mismatch between two source files (meta.json uses -5~+5, nodes.json uses -1~+1) and standardizes on nodes.json as the canonical scale.

nodes.name: product-positioning.md
nodes.summary: States the dual-audience product positioning (researchers vs. game developers) and derives a two-layer interface split — a Research Observatory frontend and a raw SDK/API layer — plus a disclaimer policy gated on an isRealPerson flag for ethical boundaries.

nodes.name: time-model.md
nodes.summary: Defines the three-layer time hierarchy (tick/session/cycle) with event-driven session closure and a hard tick cap, plus a per-topic exponential stance-decay formula (STANCE_LAMBDA) that lets core ideology decay slowly while tactical positions swing quickly.
nodes.languageNotes: v2 revision superseding design-priority-three.md §2's fixed-length session scheme; ticks are explicitly decoupled from wall-clock time to guarantee reproducibility given a seed and input sequence.
